ksa_text: Knowledge of change management frameworks (Prosci ADKAR, Kotter) and how to adapt them for data and AI capability deployments with heterogeneous user populations.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of resource management principles and techniques.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Knowledge of resource management principles and techniques.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Operations & Program Management KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.414) to justify inclusion for the Director of Data Science. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world Director of Data Science roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Recent job postings for Director of Data Science roles increasingly include Operations & Program Management competencies as organizations broaden role scope. External evidence supports inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Overturn: evolving market expectations for Director of Data Science now include Operations & Program Management competencies. Revised to include.

decision_rationale: Initially excluded but overturned through adversarial review. Operations & Program Management knowledge KSA is relevant to the Director of Data Science based on external evidence and internal consistency re-evaluation.
